key_insight: The paper turns reinforcement learning for discrete diffusion models into a continuous-time stochastic control problem, enabling policy-gradient fine-tuning with intermediate rewards along the denoising trajectory.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical way to post-train discrete diffusion and masked diffusion language models using reward signals that are not differentiable, including reasoning- and code-focused optimization.
method: Model the discrete state evolution as a controlled continuous-time Markov chain (CTMC).
Derive continuous-time policy-gradient updates, including PPO- and GRPO-style objectives.
Use trajectory subsampling to estimate expensive likelihood ratios in masked diffusion LLMs.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation on low-dimensional entropy-regularized optimization problems and RL post-training of discrete diffusion LLMs for mathematical reasoning and coding tasks.
watch_item: Computing per-position trajectory likelihoods can still be expensive, so the subsampling approximation may introduce variance or estimation error in large models.
paper_title: A Continuous-Time Reinforcement Learning Framework for Fine-Tuning Discrete Diffusion Models
